title: 9 students brought to hospital after school bus crash in Everett
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Nine students taken to hospitals for observation after bus crash in Everett, no serious injuries reported.
Crash involved a school bus and a black car at Broadway and Langton Street, according to authorities.
Surveillance footage shows the black car striking the side of the school bus, investigation is ongoing.

title: Huge endangered snapping turtle, somehow loose in Mass., rescued from pond
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: An alligator snapping turtle was found in an Essex County pond, marking one of the first sightings in Massachusetts.
The 60-pound turtle, suffering from pneumonia, was rescued and taken to the New England Wildlife Center for recovery.
The turtle likely abandoned by its owner, originated from warmer regions like Louisiana and can't thrive in Massachusetts.

title: After state trooper killed, Senate approves amendment aimed at deterring wrong-way drivers
author: Molly Farrar
bullets: Massachusetts Senate voted 40-0 to implement new measures to prevent wrong-way driving incidents on highways.
Amendment mandates MassDOT to install multi-sensory warning devices and detection systems for wrong-way drivers.
State Trooper Kevin Trainor and 50-year-old Hernan Marrero both died in a head-on collision on Route 1.

title: Thoughts on owning one gas and one electric vehicle
author: John Paul
bullets: AAA Northeast's Car Doctor suggests combining a gasoline and electric vehicle for travel flexibility.
The Hyundai Ioniq models are praised, while the Tesla Model 3 is not yet fully evaluated by the Car Doctor.
Home charging is essential for electric vehicle convenience, with options like the MSI Smart Charger available.

title: Red Sox top prospect Franklin Arias makes major leap in latest ESPN rankings
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Franklin Arias jumps from No. 36 to No. 6 in ESPN’s top 50 prospect rankings.
Arias is batting .328 with eight home runs and 25 RBI for Double-A Portland this season.
Signed in 2023, Arias was initially valued for defense but improved offensively, raising his prospect status.

title: Police respond to threat made against Perkins School for the Blind
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Watertown police responded to an online threat against Perkins School for the Blind Thursday morning, prompting a lockdown.
Police and staff quickly assessed the threat; no criminal charges will be filed, and the situation is resolved.
Due to privacy concerns, Watertown police will not release further information about the incident at the school.

title: Second arrest made in alleged sexual abuse of dog in Connecticut
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Connecticut police arrested Damon Mesite, 28, for aiding and abetting sexual assault of an animal on May 8.
Kristiina Kennedy, 45, was previously charged for sexually assaulting her 2-year-old pitbull mix and creating pornography.
Investigators found additional evidence on Kennedy's devices suggesting sexual contact with her two cats.

title: Bruins reportedly moving on from one of their assistant coaches
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Bruins are reportedly not renewing assistant coach Jay Leach's contract this offseason.
Leach, 46, rejoined the Bruins in 2024 and oversaw the defense and penalty kill under Marco Sturm.
Boston's defense ranked 30th in high-danger chances allowed and 24th in penalty kill success last season.

title: NFL insider would be ‘surprised’ if Patriots don’t land A.J. Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: NFL insider Ian Rapoport suggests he'd be surprised if A.J. Brown doesn't join the Patriots.
Rapoport highlights that a trade might involve Patriots' receiver Kayshon Boutte.
The Patriots seek a favorable draft pick deal by June 1, differing from the Eagles' demands.

title: Here’s how much a last-minute trip to Cape Cod for Memorial Day weekend costs
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: A one-day Cape Cod trip for Memorial Day costs approximately $162.75, including gas, beach parking, and meals.
A couple's getaway from Friday to Monday totals around $1,867.55, factoring in lodging, meals, and entertainment.
A family vacation for four over Memorial Day weekend costs about $2,331.35, including hotel, meals, and activities.
